title: Constrained postmortem rewrite
response:
CHECKOUT PAYMENT FAILURES — POSTMORTEM SUMMARY

On Tuesday at 14:05 UTC, checkout began failing when the TLS certificate on the payment gateway expired unnoticed. For 41 minutes, about 12% of checkout attempts failed. The team restored service by renewing the certificate. To prevent recurrence, the team decided to implement automated certificate renewal and alerts that fire 30 days before any certificate expiry.
scoreDetail: constraint compliance: 9/10, fact preservation: 9/10, professional clarity: 8/10, concision: 7/10 — Fully compliant with all hard constraints and facts, but the added header line and extra 'restored service' sentence are unrequested padding that cost concision points.
